tool.name: palindrome
tool.summary: report exact reverse-complement palindromic regions in nucleotide sequences

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `palindrome` reports exact reverse-complement palindromic windows in nucleotide sequence records. EMBOSS-RS v1 searches the forward source sequence only and emits every exact palindrome within the configured inclusive length range.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local nucleotide input plus optional minimum and maximum palindrome lengths. Protein and unknown-classified inputs are rejected because reverse-complement semantics are required.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The result is a stable table with record identifier, 1-based inclusive start and end coordinates, window length, and the matched palindromic sequence.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs palindrome`. Validation currently covers exact 6-residue palindrome reporting against a committed nucleotide FASTA fixture through the Rust tool and service layers.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not collapse nested palindromes, does not score imperfect palindromes, and does not search reverse-strand coordinates separately from the forward source sequence.

artifacts.label: Palindrome nucleotide FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed nucleotide FASTA fixture containing exact 6-residue palindromic windows.

examples.title: Report exact 6-residue palindromic windows
examples.description: Runs `palindrome` against a committed nucleotide FASTA fixture with both minimum and maximum length set to 6.

expected_outputs.label: Exact palindrome hit table
expected_outputs.description: Two exact 6-residue palindrome rows are reported with stable 1-based inclusive coordinates.
